title: Focus Mode Switch
prompt:
Create a production-ready Focus Mode Switch using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original preference control switches between a calm Focus mode and a collaborative Shared mode. A mint thumb slides between two clearly named positions, while a compact preference card changes its title and explanatory sentence. The meaning must be clear without relying solely on the thumb position or color. Use a light Bento composition with #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, a very soft neutral shadow, #111111 primary text, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Mint is the only bright accent and no card surface may use a gradient.

Use one native button with role switch, aria-checked, a 44px minimum target, and an aria-live polite status message. Click, Enter, and Space must toggle the mode safely. Animate the thumb with a spring using stiffness 360 and damping 28, and fade the supporting sentence over 150ms ease-out; use a 90ms press scale of 0.98. Keep all state local and deterministic, with no timers, random values, browser storage, remote assets, network requests, or external side effects.

At 320px, allow the text to stack beside or below the control without clipping; in a detail preview, permit a comfortable maximum width. Preserve logical focus order, visible 3px focus rings, AA contrast, and labels that explicitly state the active mode. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the thumb and retaining only a brief opacity fade. Return complete paste-ready client code, a runnable pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [f,setF]=React.useState(true);return
WORKSPACE MODE

{f?'Focus mode':'Shared mode'}

{f?'Notifications are grouped for uninterrupted work.':'Updates remain visible for shared coordination.'}
}return ;})()
